Many asset formats describe a hierarchy of transforms that model the spatial relationships between meshes, lights, and cameras in the asset. If the object’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/children] property is `nil`, this method sets that property to a new [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObjectContainer] instance before adding the child object.

# addChild(_:)

**Instance Method**

Adds a child object to this object, creating a container for the object’s children if necessary.

## Declaration

```swift
func addChild(_ child: MDLObject)
```

## Parameters

- **child**: The child object to add.

## Discussion

Many asset formats describe a hierarchy of transforms that model the spatial relationships between meshes, lights, and cameras in the asset. In such formats, hierarchic transforms are [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject] instances whose [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/children] property references the objects affected by the transform—and those objects can be other [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject] instances with children of their own.

If the object’s [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/children] property is `nil`, this method sets that property to a new [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObjectContainer] instance before adding the child object.

## Working with Object Hierarchies

- **parent**: The parent object that contains this object.
- **children**: A component that manages this object’s collection of children.
- **enumerateChildObjects(of:root:using:stopPointer:)**: Executes the specified block using each object in this object’s child hierarchy.
- **path**: A path that identifies the object in an asset’s object hierarchy using object names.
- **atPath(_:)**: Returns the child object at the specified path.
